title: Nervilia campestris
intro: Nervilia campestris, also known as the grassy nervilia or grassy bonnet orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. Traditionally, Nervilia campestris has been used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Nervilia campestris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nervilia campestris is often prepared as a decoction or infusion to treat ailments, and it is also used in the production of traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
taxonomy: Nervilia campestris is a species of orchid in the genus Nervilia, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This classification puts Nervilia campestris in the order Asparagales. The family Orchidaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. Within the family Orchidaceae, Nervilia campestris is a member of the subfamily Orchidoideae. The order Asparagales is a group of flowering plants that also includes lilies and asparagus.
